title: Anatomy & Physiology — USMLE High-Yield
description: Embryology, gross anatomy, histology, and physiology essentials most commonly tested on USMLE Step 1.
subject: medicine

cards.front: Brachial plexus: roots, trunks, divisions, cords, branches (mnemonic)
cards.back: Real Texans Drink Cold Beer — Roots (C5-T1), Trunks (upper/middle/lower), Divisions, Cords (lateral/posterior/medial), Branches (musculocutaneous, axillary, radial, median, ulnar)

cards.front: Erb's palsy: root and position
cards.back: Upper brachial plexus injury (C5–C6) — shoulder dystocia or downward traction. 'Waiter's tip' position: arm adducted, internally rotated, forearm pronated, wrist flexed

cards.front: Klumpke palsy: root and position
cards.back: Lower brachial plexus injury (C8–T1) — upward traction (breech delivery, grabbing to stop fall). Intrinsic hand muscle weakness, claw hand, Horner syndrome (T1 sympathetics)

cards.front: Median nerve injury at wrist: findings
cards.back: Carpal tunnel: lateral 3½ digits paresthesias/weakness, thenar wasting. 'Ape hand' (unable to oppose thumb). Cannot make OK sign

cards.front: Ulnar nerve injury at medial epicondyle: findings
cards.back: 'Claw hand' (hyperextension of 4th/5th MCPs, flexion of 4th/5th IPJs due to loss of intrinsics). Loss of medial 1½ digits sensation. Wasting of hypothenar eminence and interossei

cards.front: Radial nerve injury: findings by level
cards.back: Saturday night palsy (spiral groove): wrist drop + loss of finger extension + sensory loss dorsal hand. Posterior interosseous nerve (deep branch): finger drop only, no sensory loss, wrist extension preserved (ECRB intact)

cards.front: Femoral nerve injury: findings
cards.back: L2–L4. Loss of knee extension (quadriceps), loss of hip flexion, loss of patellar reflex. Sensory loss: anterior thigh and medial leg

cards.front: Sciatic nerve injury: findings
cards.back: L4–S3. Weak knee flexion (hamstrings). All foot/ankle movements lost. Loss of ankle jerk. Foot drop if common peroneal component predominant

cards.front: Common peroneal (fibular) nerve injury: findings
cards.back: Foot drop (loss of dorsiflexion and eversion), sensory loss lateral leg and dorsum of foot. Injured at fibular head (fracture or pressure)

cards.front: Obturator nerve injury: findings
cards.back: L2–L4. Loss of hip adduction and rotation. Sensory loss medial thigh. Injured in pelvic surgery/malignancy

cards.front: Appendix: surface anatomy location
cards.back: McBurney's point = 1/3 of the way from ASIS to umbilicus. Rovsing's sign: palpation of LLQ causes pain in RLQ. Psoas sign, obturator sign in appendicitis

cards.front: Inguinal canal boundaries and contents
cards.back: Floor: inguinal ligament. Roof: transversalis fascia + internal oblique. Anterior: external oblique. Posterior: transversalis fascia. Contents: spermatic cord (male) / round ligament of uterus (female)

cards.front: Indirect vs direct inguinal hernia
cards.back: Indirect: through internal inguinal ring → lateral to inferior epigastric vessels. Congenital (patent processus vaginalis). Direct: through Hesselbach's triangle (weakness in abdominal wall) → medial to inferior epigastrics

cards.front: Hesselbach's triangle boundaries
cards.back: Inferior epigastric artery (lateral), inguinal ligament (inferior), rectus abdominis (medial)

cards.front: Femoral triangle: borders and contents
cards.back: Superior: inguinal ligament. Medial: adductor longus. Lateral: sartorius. Contents (lateral to medial): NAVEL — Nerve, Artery, Vein, Empty space, Lymphatics. Femoral hernia = medial to vein

cards.front: Coronary arteries: RCA and LCA territories
cards.back: RCA: SA node, AV node (in most), RV, posterior LV. LCA → LAD: anterior septum, anterior LV, apex. LCA → LCx: lateral LV, posterior LV (in left-dominant circulation)

cards.front: Aortic arch branches (order)
cards.back: Brachiocephalic trunk (→ right subclavian + right common carotid), Left common carotid, Left subclavian. 'Big Loud Strong' or 'B-L-S'

cards.front: Circle of Willis: components
cards.back: Two internal carotid arteries → ACAs (connected by AComm A) + MCAs; basilar artery → two PCAs (connected to ICAs by PComm As). Anterior circulation (70%) vs posterior (30%)

cards.front: Portal hypertension: portosystemic anastomoses
cards.back: Esophageal varices (left gastric → esophageal → azygous), hemorrhoids (superior rectal → inferior rectal), caput medusae (umbilical veins → abdominal wall), splenorenal

cards.front: Foregut derivatives: blood supply
cards.back: Celiac artery. Structures: lower esophagus, stomach, proximal duodenum (to major papilla), liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen

cards.front: Midgut derivatives: blood supply
cards.back: SMA (superior mesenteric artery). Structures: small intestine (distal duodenum, jejunum, ileum), cecum, appendix, ascending colon, proximal 2/3 transverse colon

cards.front: Hindgut derivatives: blood supply
cards.back: IMA (inferior mesenteric artery). Structures: distal 1/3 transverse colon, descending colon, sigmoid, rectum, upper anal canal

cards.front: Cardiac embryology: ventricular septal defect (VSD) — embryologic origin
cards.back: Most VSDs are in the membranous septum (neural crest cells + endocardial cushion contribution). Muscular VSDs in the trabecula carneae

cards.front: Neural tube defects: causes and prevention
cards.back: Folate deficiency. Anencephaly (failure of anterior neuropore), spina bifida (failure of posterior neuropore). AFP elevated in maternal serum and amniotic fluid (also elevated in gastroschisis). Folic acid supplementation 1 month before conception + first trimester

cards.front: TORCH infections: Toxoplasma, Other, Rubella, CMV, HSV
cards.back: T: chorioretinitis, hydrocephalus, intracranial calcifications. R: sensorineural deafness, PDA, cataracts. C: periventricular calcifications, sensorineural deafness. H: vesicular skin lesions, encephalitis. O: syphilis — bone changes, snuffles, CN VIII deafness

cards.front: Frank-Starling curve: shift left vs right
cards.back: Left shift (↑ contractility): positive inotropes, sympathetic stimulation. Right shift (↓ contractility): heart failure, negative inotropes (β-blockers, CCBs)

cards.front: Pressure-volume loop: changes in preload vs afterload
cards.back: ↑ Preload: loop shifts right (↑ EDV). ↑ Afterload: loop shifts left (↑ systolic pressure, ↓ stroke volume). ↑ Contractility: ↑ stroke volume without change in EDV

cards.front: What is the difference between dead space and shunt?
cards.back: Dead space: ventilated but not perfused (V/Q → infinity). Shunt: perfused but not ventilated (V/Q → 0). Shunt does NOT correct with supplemental O2

cards.front: Glomerular filtration: forces (Starling forces in glomerulus)
cards.back: GFR depends on: Kf × (Pgc − Pbs − πgc + πbs). Pgc = glomerular capillary hydrostatic pressure, Pbs = Bowman's space, πgc = oncotic pressure. GFR ↑ with ↑ Pgc (dilation of afferent) or ↑ Kf

cards.front: Effect of ACE inhibitor on GFR in renal artery stenosis
cards.back: ACE inhibitor dilates efferent arteriole → ↓ glomerular hydrostatic pressure → ↓ GFR → AKI in patients with bilateral RAS or solitary kidney with RAS

cards.front: Testicular torsion: age group and management
cards.back: Adolescents (peak 13–14 years). 'Bell clapper' deformity. 'Sunrise' or horizontal lie. Sudden onset scrotal pain, absent cremasteric reflex. Management: IMMEDIATE surgical detorsion (< 6 hours for salvage)

cards.front: Cryptorchidism: complications
cards.back: Infertility (bilateral), increased risk testicular cancer (seminoma) even in contralateral descended testis. Treatment: orchiopexy before age 2

cards.front: Surface anatomy: where is the femoral vein for central access?
cards.back: Medial to femoral artery in femoral triangle (remember NAVEL). Approach below inguinal ligament

cards.front: Thoracic duct: origin and termination
cards.back: Arises from cisterna chyli (L1–L2) → enters thorax → ascends to drain into junction of left subclavian and left internal jugular veins. Carries chyle from abdomen/pelvis/lower extremities/left thorax/left head and neck

cards.front: Structures injured in mastectomy: nerves to avoid
cards.back: Long thoracic nerve (serratus anterior — winged scapula if injured), thoracodorsal nerve (latissimus dorsi), intercostobrachial nerve (medial arm sensation)

cards.front: Cardiac action potential phases (ventricular myocyte)
cards.back: Phase 0: rapid depolarization (voltage-gated Na+ channels). Phase 1: partial repolarization (K+ channels). Phase 2: plateau (Ca2+ influx = K+ efflux). Phase 3: rapid repolarization (K+ channels). Phase 4: resting (-90 mV)

cards.front: Renal blood flow: arteries in order from aorta to glomerulus
cards.back: Renal artery → interlobar → arcuate → interlobular → afferent arteriole → glomerular capillaries → efferent arteriole → peritubular capillaries (or vasa recta in medulla)

cards.front: Layers of the abdominal wall (lateral to medial, outside to inside)
cards.back: Skin → subcutaneous fat → Scarpa's fascia → external oblique → internal oblique → transversus abdominis → transversalis fascia → preperitoneal fat → parietal peritoneum

cards.front: Diaphragm openings and their contents
cards.back: T8: IVC + right phrenic nerve. T10: esophagus + vagal trunks + left gastric vessels. T12: aorta + thoracic duct + azygous vein. Mnemonic: 'I 8 (ate) 10 Eggs At 12'

cards.front: Knee ligament injuries: mechanism
cards.back: ACL: valgus + external rotation (dashboard injury). PCL: posterior force on flexed knee. MCL: valgus force. LCL: varus force. Unhappy triad: ACL + MCL + medial meniscus

cards.front: Compartment syndrome: nerve and vessel at risk in anterior leg compartment
cards.back: Deep peroneal nerve (foot drop) + anterior tibial artery. Hallmark: pain out of proportion, pain with passive stretch. Emergent fasciotomy

cards.front: Thoracic duct: course and drainage
cards.back: Originates at cisterna chyli (L1–L2). Travels through aortic hiatus (T12), ascends right of aorta, crosses midline at T5, empties into left subclavian–internal jugular junction. Drains: lower limbs, abdomen, left thorax, left head/neck, left arm. Right lymphatic duct drains right upper quadrant.

cards.front: Recurrent laryngeal nerve: course and injury consequences
cards.back: Right RLN: loops under subclavian artery. Left RLN: loops under aortic arch at ligamentum arteriosum. Injury (thyroidectomy, mediastinal mass): hoarseness, ipsilateral vocal cord paralysis. Both injured: respiratory distress. Superior laryngeal nerve (external branch): cricothyroid muscle → pitch changes.

cards.front: Femoral triangle: boundaries and contents (NAVEL)
cards.back: Borders: inguinal ligament (superior), sartorius (lateral), adductor longus (medial), iliopsoas + pectineus (floor). Contents lateral→medial: Nerve (femoral), Artery (femoral), Vein (femoral), Empty space, Lymphatics. Femoral hernias enter medial to vein → femoral canal.

cards.front: Brachial plexus: roots, trunks, divisions, cords, branches (5 Rs Too Dirty For me)
cards.back: Roots C5–T1 → 3 Trunks (upper C5-6, middle C7, lower C8-T1) → anterior/posterior Divisions → 3 Cords (lateral, posterior, medial) → Branches. Long thoracic nerve (C5-7): serratus anterior. Axillary (C5-6): deltoid. Musculocutaneous (C5-7): biceps.

cards.front: Erb's palsy vs Klumpke's palsy
cards.back: Erb's (C5-6): waiter's tip position — arm adducted/internally rotated, extended elbow, pronated forearm. Caused by shoulder dystocia/lateral neck traction. Klumpke's (C8-T1): claw hand; interosseous + lumbrical weakness. Caused by forceful arm abduction. Horner syndrome if T1 rami communicantes involved.

cards.front: Parasympathetic ganglia of the head: locations and functions
cards.back: Ciliary ganglion (CN III): pupil constriction + lens accommodation. Pterygopalatine (CN VII): lacrimal + nasal glands. Submandibular (CN VII): submandibular + sublingual glands. Otic (CN IX): parotid gland. Sympathetic supply: superior cervical ganglion via carotid plexus.

cards.front: Carpal tunnel syndrome: anatomy of compression
cards.back: Carpal tunnel contents: median nerve + 9 flexor tendons (FDS×4, FDP×4, FPL). Roof = flexor retinaculum. Median nerve compression → paresthesias of thumb/index/middle/radial ring fingers + thenar wasting. Palmar cutaneous branch of median (arises proximal to tunnel) is SPARED → palm sensation intact.

cards.front: Rotator cuff muscles and their actions (SITS)
cards.back: Supraspinatus: abduction initiation (0–15°; tested with empty can test). Infraspinatus: external rotation. Teres minor: external rotation. Subscapularis: internal rotation (lift-off test). Most commonly torn: supraspinatus. Nerve: suprascapular (supra + infra), lower subscapular (sub + teres minor).

cards.front: Circle of Willis: named components and common aneurysm sites
cards.back: Anterior: 2× ACA + anterior communicating (AComm). Middle: 2× MCA. Posterior: 2× PCA + 2× posterior communicating (PComm) + basilar + 2× vertebral. Aneurysm sites: AComm (most common, frontal lobe deficits), PComm (CN III palsy — down-and-out pupil), MCA bifurcation (contralateral face/arm weakness).
